SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.util.optimizer

Class ProcessModelOptimizationView

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.process.SimulationBaseClass
neqsim.process.processmodel.ProcessSystem
neqsim.process.util.optimizer.ProcessModelOptimizationView
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, SimulationInterface, NamedInterface
public class ProcessModelOptimizationView extends ProcessSystem
Read-through adapter that presents a multi-area ProcessModel as a single ProcessSystem so that the existing ProductionOptimizer engine can optimize a whole plant without any change to its proven single-ProcessSystem search and evaluation logic.

The adapter overrides only the three operations the optimizer engine performs on a ProcessSystem:

  • run(UUID) — delegates to ProcessModel.runUntilConverged(int, double) so cross-area recycles and shared streams converge between iterations (instead of a single ProcessSystem.run() pass).
  • getUnitOperations() — returns the units of every area concatenated in area insertion order, so the optimizer's capacity / bottleneck scan covers the entire plant.
  • getConstrainedEquipment() — aggregates capacity-constrained equipment across all areas.

Because the inherited ProcessSystem.getUnit(String) is implemented in terms of getUnitOperations(), manipulated-variable setters and objective / constraint functions written against the ProcessSystem API (for example proc.getUnit("Compressor")) resolve units across all areas with no API change. Area-qualified addresses of the form "Area::Unit" are also supported.

This class is purely additive: it does not modify ProcessModel or ProcessSystem. The adapter is a read-through view — it never adds units to its own internal list and never calls super.run().

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team
See Also:
  • ProductionOptimizer
  • ProcessModel

  • Constructor Details

    • ProcessModelOptimizationView

      public ProcessModelOptimizationView(ProcessModel model)
      Creates a view over the given model using robust default convergence settings (50 iterations, tolerance 5e-3, which is robust for plants with near-zero-flow anti-surge recycles).
      Parameters:
      model - the multi-area plant to optimize (must not be null)

    • ProcessModelOptimizationView

      public ProcessModelOptimizationView(ProcessModel model, int maxIterations, double tolerance)
      Creates a view over the given model with explicit convergence settings.
      Parameters:
      model - the multi-area plant to optimize (must not be null)
      maxIterations - maximum cross-area convergence iterations (must be greater than zero)
      tolerance - convergence tolerance for runUntilConverged (must be greater than zero)

  • Method Details

    • getModel

      public ProcessModel getModel()
      Returns the multi-area plant backing this view.
      Returns:
      the wrapped ProcessModel

    • run

      public void run(UUID id)
      Runs the whole plant to cross-area convergence instead of performing a single ProcessSystem.run() pass.
      Specified by:
      run in interface SimulationInterface
      Overrides:
      run in class ProcessSystem
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier (unused; convergence is managed by the model)

    • getUnitOperations

      public List<ProcessEquipmentInterface> getUnitOperations()
      Returns the unit operations of every area, concatenated in area insertion order.
      Overrides:
      getUnitOperations in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      an aggregated list of all unit operations across the plant

    • getConstrainedEquipment

      public List<CapacityConstrainedEquipment> getConstrainedEquipment()
      Returns capacity-constrained equipment aggregated across every area.
      Overrides:
      getConstrainedEquipment in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      an aggregated list of capacity-constrained equipment across the plant

    • getUnit

      public ProcessEquipmentInterface getUnit(String name)
      Resolves a unit by name across all areas. Area-qualified addresses of the form "Area::Unit" are resolved against the named area first; plain names fall back to a plant-wide search via the inherited ProcessSystem.getUnit(String) (which iterates getUnitOperations()).
      Overrides:
      getUnit in class ProcessSystem
      Parameters:
      name - the unit name, optionally area-qualified as "Area::Unit"
      Returns:
      the matching unit, or null if no unit matches

    • getBottleneck

      public ProcessEquipmentInterface getBottleneck()

      getBottleneck.

      Identifies the equipment with the highest capacity utilization. This method checks both:

      • Traditional capacity: getCapacityDuty() / getCapacityMax()
      • Multi-constraint capacity: Equipment implementing CapacityConstrainedEquipment
      Overrides:
      getBottleneck in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      a ProcessEquipmentInterface object representing the bottleneck, or null if no equipment has capacity defined

    • getBottleneckUtilization

      public double getBottleneckUtilization()
      Gets the utilization ratio of the bottleneck equipment.
      Overrides:
      getBottleneckUtilization in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      utilization as fraction (1.0 = 100%), or 0.0 if no bottleneck found

    • findBottleneck

      public BottleneckResult findBottleneck()
      Finds the process bottleneck with detailed constraint information.

      This method extends ProcessSystem.getBottleneck() by returning detailed information about which specific constraint is limiting the bottleneck equipment. Uses the universal constraint API on ProcessEquipmentInterface so ALL equipment types are checked, not just those implementing CapacityConstrainedEquipment.

      For simple bottleneck detection without constraint details, use ProcessSystem.getBottleneck().

      Overrides:
      findBottleneck in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      BottleneckResult containing the bottleneck equipment, limiting constraint, and utilization; returns empty result if no constrained equipment found
      See Also:
      • ProcessSystem.getBottleneck()

    • isAnyEquipmentOverloaded

      public boolean isAnyEquipmentOverloaded()
      Checks if any equipment in the process is overloaded (exceeds design capacity).

      All equipment with capacity constraints is checked, not just those implementing CapacityConstrainedEquipment. Only equipment with capacity analysis enabled is checked.

      Overrides:
      isAnyEquipmentOverloaded in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      true if any equipment has capacity utilization above 100%

    • isAnyHardLimitExceeded

      public boolean isAnyHardLimitExceeded()
      Checks if any equipment exceeds a HARD capacity limit.

      HARD limits represent absolute equipment limits that cannot be exceeded without trip or damage, such as maximum compressor speed or surge limits. All equipment is checked.

      Overrides:
      isAnyHardLimitExceeded in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      true if any HARD constraint is exceeded

    • getUtilizationSnapshotJson

      public String getUtilizationSnapshotJson()
      Returns a stable, side-effect-free JSON utilization snapshot of every unit in this process system.

      This is the recommended observation endpoint for machine-learning / reinforcement-learning optimization loops. It reports, for every unit, the maximum capacity utilization, the limiting constraint name, a per-constraint breakdown, feasibility, and (for compressors and pumps) the shaft power. The process-wide bottleneck and anyOverloaded flags summarise the whole flowsheet. Schema is versioned by schemaVersion ("1.0").

      The method does not run the flowsheet; call SimulationInterface.run() (or ProcessAutomation.evaluate(java.util.Map<java.lang.String, java.lang.Double>, java.lang.String, java.util.List<java.lang.String>, java.lang.String, int, double)) first so the reported utilization reflects the latest setpoints.

      Overrides:
      getUtilizationSnapshotJson in class ProcessSystem
      Returns:
      JSON string {schemaVersion, name, units:[...], bottleneck:{...}, anyOverloaded, anyHardLimitExceeded}
